metadata.title: The Dollar Canon — AI+1 series 2/4
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Is the textbook really a dollar? Yes — a dollar, on purpose. Here is why the canonical version of every course is priced like a coffee tip, and what you actually get for it.

props.command: is the textbook really a dollar?

beats.narration_text: One dollar on Kindle — free if you have Kindle Unlimited. Every course. And it is the full canonical textbook, not a teaser chapter with an upsell at the end.

props.heading: $1. The whole book.
props.body:
One dollar on Kindle.
Free on Kindle Unlimited.

Full canonical textbook — not a teaser chapter.
No upsell at the end.

beats.narration_text: The lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube — the complete course in video, public. Assign it to a class, binge it yourself, check our teaching before you spend anything.

props.heading: The course is watchable, free.
props.body:
Complete course on the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
Public. No account required.

Assign it. Audit it. Check the teaching before you spend anything.

beats.narration_text: The slide decks run on our site as voiced, animated pages — every deck narrated, every build animated, right in the browser. No download, no license key, no viewer to install.

props.heading: Decks that talk.
props.body:
Every deck voiced and animated in the browser.
No download. No license key. No viewer to install.

The full teaching surface, free.

beats.narration_text: So predict the business logic: why would we price the canon at a dollar, when the custom versions are what pay for the work? Take a second.

props.command: why price the canon at a dollar?

beats.narration_text: Because the canon is the audit. You can read the book, watch the course, and hear the decks before you ever email us — so when you buy a custom version, you know exactly what the base is. No demo call. And we are a five-oh-one-C-three: on the canon, reach matters more than margin.

props.heading: The canon is the audit.
props.body:
Read the book. Watch the course. Hear the decks.
All before you email us.

When you buy custom, you know exactly what the base is.
No demo call. 501(c)(3): reach matters more than margin.

beats.narration_text: One page. A dollar buys the audit: the full book, the full videos, the talking decks. Read the base before you ever pay for a version of it.

artifactLines: $1 Kindle textbook (free on Kindle Unlimited) — the full canonical text, every course.
The complete course in video, free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
Every slide deck voiced and animated on the site — nothing to install.
Phase gate: audit the canon first · buy custom only when the base has earned it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — audit us. Paste this into Claude, pick a course, and check the book against the videos. If the canon holds up, the custom conversation is worth your time.

props.command: Pick one Humanitarians AI course. Pull its $1 Kindle textbook and its free YouTube playlist, and check the chapters against the videos — does the coverage match?

beats.narration_text: The dollar canon. Next: your version.
